task_completion.description: Open the scolv application and load the 2024 Noto Peninsula earthquake event.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SeisComP 'scolv' GUI is open, and the event list or summary tab shows the 2024 Noto Peninsula earthquake (M7.5, 2024-01-01) selected.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the waveform picker and display the waveforms for the target stations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The waveform picker window/tab is open, and the station codes GE.GSI, GE.BKB, and GE.SANI are visible next to the seismogram traces.

task_completion.description: Manually adjust or add P-wave arrival picks for the target stations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Vertical pick markers (typically labeled 'P') are visible on the waveform traces for GE.GSI, GE.BKB, and/or GE.SANI, indicating the agent has interacted with the arrivals.

task_completion.description: Relocate the hypocenter based on the refined picks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Location tab/window is active, showing updated coordinates (latitude/longitude) or residuals, and the 'Relocate' button appears to have been clicked.

task_completion.description: Commit the updated origin to the database with the evaluation mode set to 'manual'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A commit dialog or action is visible in the scolv GUI, and there is a dropdown, checkbox, or text indicating the evaluation mode is set to 'manual' before or during the commit.

integrity.description: The agent used the scolv GUI to perform the task rather than command-line shortcuts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show genuine interaction with the scolv graphical interface (clicking tabs, moving pick markers, clicking commit) rather than terminal windows executing SQL queries or Python scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with actual seismological waveform data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The picker window displays real seismogram traces (wavy lines representing seismic data) for the specified stations, not empty windows or mock data.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a database client to bypass the GUI commit process.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows or database clients (like DBeaver or mysql CLI) running commands like 'UPDATE Origin SET evaluationMode="manual"'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target event is the 2024 Noto Peninsula, Japan Earthquake (2024-01-01, M7.5). The agent must use the 'scolv' GUI application. The target stations for P-wave picking are GE.GSI, GE.BKB, and GE.SANI. The agent must relocate the hypocenter and commit the new origin to the database with the evaluation mode strictly set to 'manual'.
